title: Puerto Banus Dolphin Watching: Boat Hire Costs & Tips for 2026
html:
Set off from Puerto Banus marina in a private motorboat and you can reach dolphin grounds off the Costa del Sol in under 20 minutes. Shared tours start at around EUR 55 per person and exclusive charters from EUR 350 for a half-day, with options listed on BoatHire24 to suit most budgets.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most dolphin-watching departures happen from Puerto Banus marina itself, just west of Marbella. You can also launch from Marbella marina to the east or Estepona marina further west. The Bay of Gibraltar and the Strait of Gibraltar are the richest dolphin grounds, about 50 nautical miles south-west. Many skippers head towards Isla de Tarifa or the Punta de Europa lighthouse, where common and striped dolphins gather year-round. You can compare verified boat listings in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent for dolphin watching

  1. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB), Fast and agile for chasing pods, best for 4 to 6 guests, from EUR 90 per hour.
  2. Motor yacht, Comfortable and stable, suitable for 8 to 12 guests, from EUR 500 per half-day.
  3. Catamaran, Spacious deck and low wake, ideal for families of up to 14 people, from EUR 800 per half-day.
  4. Sailboat, Quiet approach and eco-friendly, perfect for a relaxed day for up to 6 guests, from EUR 350 per half-day.

To check specific models such as the Limpopo for hire, an Axopar 28 with a 250hp engine, you can browse the inventory online; it is a popular choice for fast trips to the dolphin feeding areas. One option worth a look is the Limpopo for hire.

How much does it cost to rent a boat for dolphin watching in Puerto Banus?

Prices vary by boat size and duration, with shared tours starting at EUR 55 per person and private charters from EUR 350. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

  • Shared dolphin-watching tour (2 to 3 hours): EUR 55 to EUR 80 per person.
  • Small motorboat private hire (half-day, 4 hours): EUR 350 to EUR 550 for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran private full-day (8 hours): EUR 1,200 to EUR 2,000 for up to 14 guests.
  • Luxury crewed yacht (full-day, with skipper and crew): EUR 2,500 to EUR 4,500 for up to 12 guests.

You can also take a look at the booking FAQ to understand cancellation policies and deposit requirements before you confirm. To see how booking works, visit the booking FAQ.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 55, EUR 4,500
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2 to 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 4 to 14 persons per boat
  • Currency: EUR

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

In Spain, you generally need a recreational boating licence (such as the PER) for any boat over a certain power or length, but many charters include a qualified skipper.The specific licence rules depend on engine power and distance from shore; for example, a RIB with a 250hp engine without a licence would require a skipper. Always confirm with the operator before booking. One local tip:

Dolphin activity peaks in the morning around 9-11 am, so aim for an early departure from Puerto Banus to increase your chances of seeing them.
The high season runs from June through September, when the Levante wind can create choppy conditions in the afternoon, so morning slots are advisable. In 2026, you can expect similar patterns.

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Puerto Banus?

Yes, you can rent a boat with a skipper included, which covers all safety and navigation requirements, and no licence is needed from you for that arrangement.

What types of dolphins are commonly seen near Puerto Banus?

The most common species are bottlenose, striped, and common dolphins, often spotted in pods of 10 to 50 animals in the Bay of Gibraltar.

How long does a typical dolphin-watching trip last?

Most excursions run for 2 to 3 hours, though full-day charter options of 6 to 8 hours are available for those wanting more time at sea.

Is it better to book a private charter or join a group tour?

A private charter gives you flexibility and privacy, whereas a group tour is more budget-friendly and a good way to meet other travellers.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. If you own a vessel, you can also rent out your boat through the platform and earn income when you are not using it.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
